items.title: Rohingya man pleads guilty to attack leaving countryman partially paralysed
items.summary: BUTTERWORTH: A Rohingya man was charged at the Sessions Court here today with causing grievous hurt to a countryman, resulting in the victim suffering paralysis on the right side of his body. Mohd Rofik Syukur Mohd, 39, pleaded guilty after the charge was read to him before Judge Roslan Hamid. According to the charge, Rofik is accused of intentionally causing grievous hurt to Shaiful Islam, 42, using a soil digger, an instrument which can cause death if used as a weapon.

items.title: Bus driver critical, three passengers injured after bus slams into paving machine
items.summary: BATU PAHAT: An express bus driver was critically injured after his vehicle slammed into a road paving machine at Km102.4 of the North-South Expressway northbound early today. Three passengers also sustained injuries in the 12.57am accident, while 20 others escaped unharmed. Operations commander Mohd Hafiz Othman said firefighters arrived at 1.05am and found the bus driver pinned in his seat.

items.title: MetMalaysia warns of afternoon rough weather as thunderstorms sweep across parts of peninsular Malaysia, Sabah and Sarawak
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 13 — The Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia) has issued a warning of thunderstorms, heavy rain and strong winds affecting eight states until 5pm today. In a statement today, MetMalaysia said the adverse weather conditions are expected in Perlis, Kedah (Langkawi) and Perak, involving Hulu Perak, Kuala Kangsar, Kinta, Kampar, Batang Padang and Muallim. Similar conditions are also forecast in Pahang, specifically Cameron Highlands and Bentong, as well as Hulu Selangor in Selangor.

items.title: Abd Halim Aman begins duty as new MACC chief commissioner
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA: Datuk Seri Abd Halim Aman officially reported for duty today as the new chief commissioner of the Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC). In a statement today, the MACC said Abd Halim's appointment was made through an appointment letter issued by Chief Secretary to the Government Tan Sri Shamsul Azri Abu Bakar on April 25. Abd Halim replaces Azam, who ended his tenure yesterday after serving six years as MACC chief commissioner and more than four decades in public service.

items.title: Australian real estate stocks rise as budget targets first-home buyer affordability
items.summary: The rally reflects investor optimism that the government's 2026-2027 budget, which introduced fresh housing-support measures for first-home buyers, could redirect investor demand toward new builds, supporting housing supply and construction activity. NAB analysts see investor borrowing potentially becoming more closely linked to new housing construction because negative gearing remains available for investors in new builds. Property investment firm Mirvac Group saw as much as a 3.9 per cent rise in its best intraday session since February 18, while LendLease recorded as much as a 4.6 per cent rise in its best intraday session since April 8.

items.title: Why are Britons so fed up?
items.summary: IN less than two years, Britain's governing Labour Party has gone from a landslide election victory to a historic rout at last week's local and regional votes that has put Prime Minister Keir Starmer's job on the line. While incumbents across Europe face similar difficulties, Britain's politics have become ever more febrile in the decade since it voted to leave the European Union: Starmer, its sixth leader since then, promised change but has struggled to deliver. While much of the voter anger appears to be directed at Starmer himself, opinion polls consistently point to a handful of core sources of frustration at both national and local level.

items.title: CCTV, phone videos lead to jail for repo man behind Johor Bahru paint-splash attacks
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, May 13 — A 28-year-old car repossessor was sentenced to a total of seven months in jail by two magistrates today after pleading guilty to splashing red paint on two houses in the city in separate incidents last year and in April. Muhamad Yaccob admitted to splashing red paint on a house in Taman Mount Austin on April 7 this year and on a condominium unit in Twin Danga Residensi on December 24 last year. Court documents showed CCTV footage captured Muhamad Yaccob arriving at the homes before splashing paint and fleeing.

items.title: Mainland Chinese snap up Hong Kong homes in April, most in 2 years
items.summary: HONG KONG: The number of Hong Kong homes bought by mainland Chinese surged 48 per cent in April from the previous month to a two-year high, data from property agent Midland Realty showed, supported by a strong yuan and a shift to purchasing from renting. Mainland Chinese bought 1,892 homes in April, worth HK$18.9 billion. Mainland Chinese accounted for 27.5 per cent of total housing purchases in April.

items.title: Public support for JS-SEZ hinges on jobs, housing and traffic solutions
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: Public confidence will determine the long-term success of the Johor-Singapore Special Economic Zone (JS-SEZ), according to a new study by consultancy firm Central Force International. The study, titled JS-SEZ Public Sentiment and Workforce Study, found that Johoreans broadly support the initiative, although backing remains conditional on whether the economic benefits are genuinely felt by local communities. Technology and innovation centres emerged as the top priority among respondents at 23.8 per cent, followed by industrial parks (18.6 per cent), tourism projects (18 per cent), housing and property developments (17.7 per cent), and commercial hubs (12.5 per cent).

items.title: Toddler critical after five-storey fall from JB PPR flat
items.summary: Johor Baru North police chief Assistant Commissioner Radin Ramlan Radin Taha said the boy, aged two years and 11 months, remains in critical condition following the 10.25am incident. Another neighbour, Aiman, 30, said the mother was traumatised after the fall. Shortly after, we heard the mother's screams," Aiman said.

items.title: UK's Savills expects reduced transactions due to Middle East war
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: British property advisor Savills said on Wednesday that it expects a fall and a delay in advisory transactions, weighed down by macroeconomic uncertainty arising from the Middle East conflict. In its residential transactional advisory business, the firm has seen greater caution among both buyers and sellers since the onset of the US-Iran war, both in the war-torn region and the UK, the company said. © New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

items.title: Three arrested for assaulting man at Jalan Besar Kepong restaurant
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Three men have been arrested for allegedly assaulting a man at a restaurant in Jalan Besar Kepong after accusing him of staring at them while they were eating. Sentul police chief Assistant Commissioner Basri Sagoni said the investigation paper will be referred to the deputy public prosecutor soon for further instructions. "One of the angles being investigated is whether the three men have links to secret societies," Basri said.

items.title: Serial molester who targeted women on LRT, MRT gets longer jail term, caning despite plea to return to Bangladesh
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 13 — A foreign construction worker was sentenced to 18 months in prison and one stroke of the cane by the High Court here today for outraging the modesty of five women near LRT and MRT stations in the capital last month. Justice Datuk Aslam Zainuddin imposed the sentence on Md Romjan Ali, a 41-year-old Bangladeshi national, after reviewing the May 6 decisions of four Magistrates’ Courts, which had sentenced him to six months’ imprisonment and RM14,500 fine. “For all five cases, the court will impose a global sentence.

items.title: Pulau Pangkor migrant boat capsize: Three more bodies found
items.summary: LUMUT: Three more bodies of women, believed to be victims of a boat capsize incident involving undocumented Indonesian migrants off Pulau Pangkor on Monday, have been found. Marine Police Region One Operations Command officer Deputy Superintendent Mazre Che Mahmod said the three bodies were discovered by Royal Malaysian Navy (RMN) vessel KD Sri Indera Sakti about 20 nautical miles from the location of the incident at around 6pm yesterday. The third day of the SAR operation resumed at 7am today, involving the Marine Police, RMN and the Malaysian Maritime Enforcement Agency (MMEA)," he said.

items.title: RTM to stop broadcasting channels on Astro from July 1
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Radio Televisyen Malaysia (RTM) will stop broadcasting services of its channels on Astro's satellite platform from July 1, after almost 30 years of collaboration. "Accordingly, RTM has decided to terminate the broadcasting services of RTM channels, namely TV1-HD, TV2-HD and Okey, on ASTRO's satellite platform and OTT platform operated by your party. RTM has collaborated with Astro in broadcasting TV1 and TV2 channels since 1996, and the Okey channel, formerly known as Muzik Aktif and TVi, since 2009.

items.title: Seberang Jaya assemblyman seeks probe into Penang foreign property pricing anomaly
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN: An opposition lawmaker has called on the state government to conduct a detailed investigation into what he described as "significant anomalies" in foreign residential property ownership in Penang. In contrast, he said Taiwanese expatriates in 2026 acquired 15 properties worth RM18.2 million, averaging about RM1.2 million per unit, which falls within the state's approved foreign ownership framework. "The disparity in average pricing raises serious questions over compliance with Penang's foreign property ownership guidelines and minimum price thresholds," he said today.

items.title: Three Chinese nationals linked to online scam deported to China
items.summary: Kelantan Immigration Department director Garry Gambiri said the detainees were previously arrested for offences related to online fraud activities and were also wanted by Chinese authorities. "It was carried out through close cooperation between the department, the Chinese Embassy and the Tanah Merah Immigration Depot," he said. Gambiri said 14 officers from the Tanah Merah Immigration Depot, together with three enforcement officers from the state Immigration Department, escorted the detainees from the depot to Kuala Lumpur International Airport (KLIA) to ensure the operation proceeded safely and smoothly.

items.title: No alcohol, please: Wine consumption slides in 2025 due to tariffs, fewer younger consumers
items.summary: PARIS, May 13 — Wine consumption fell worldwide last year, the industry’s trade body said Tuesday, amid changing lifestyles and economic pressure on consumers. It found consumption slid by 2.7 per cent last year to 208 million hectolitres, taking the cumulative drop since 2018 to 14 per cent. In China, wine consumption fell by 13 per cent last year and by 61 per cent since 2020.

items.title: Gerakan: High fuel usage doesn't equal wealth in Budi95 review
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The government must not assume that motorists who consume more fuel are either wasteful or wealthy, Parti Gerakan Rakyat Malaysia (Gerakan) said. Wong said Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim had first said the government had agreed in principle to review RON95 subsidies involving the wealthy, with details to be finalised soon. he said.

items.title: New MACC chief Abdul Halim starts first day in office as Azam Baki exits after 42 years
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, May 13 — Datuk Seri Abdul Halim Aman officially began his duties today as the new chief commissioner of the Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC). In a Facebook post, Chief Secretary to the Government Tan Sri Shamsul Azri Abu Bakar also congratulated Abdul Halim on his appointment. Shamsul Azri also shared photographs of Abdul Halim receiving his appointment letter as the new MACC chief commissioner.

items.title: Malaysia's MY Value Up may spur investments: JP Morgan
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia's newly-launched MY Value Up programme could create investment opportunities for investors as companies focus more on improving shareholder returns and operational performance, JP Morgan said. Malaysia announced the MY Value Up programme with the aim to shift corporate culture towards active value creation for shareholders. "Malaysia has recently taken a step up to jump on the bandwagon with the MY Value Up programme.

items.title: Pahang wants cash, not projects, for forest reserve upkeep, says MB
items.summary: KUANTAN: The Pahang government wants direct cash payments from the federal government for maintaining its forest reserves as water catchment areas instead of project-based compensation. Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Wan Rosdy Wan Ismail said Pahang had received projects worth RM72 million since 2022, but no cash compensation. He said if the federal government continues to ignore the matter, the state executive council may one day consider using parts of the state's permanent forest reserve (PFR), which covers 57 per cent of Pahang, for economic activities to generate revenue.

items.title: Penang pledges strict scrutiny for Bandar Gelugor project amid residents' protest
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN: The Penang government has pledged strict scrutiny of all development proposals to balance public feedback, technical requirements, and sustainability following protests over a project in Bandar Gelugor. State Local Government Committee chairman H'ng Mooi Lye said the state remains firm in its commitment to ensure orderly planning and full compliance with laws, guidelines, and sustainability principles. "The state government remains committed to ensuring that every proposed development is carried out in a planned and sustainable manner, in compliance with all development principles, guidelines and applicable laws," he told the state legislative assembly sitting here today.

items.title: Bursa Malaysia slips at midday on selective profit-taking
items.summary: Among the heavyweights, Malayan Banking Bhd fell four sen to RM11.20, Tenaga Nasional Bhd eased 14 sen to RM14.64, and IHH Healthcare Bhd slipped three sen to RM9.00, while Public Bank Bhd rose one sen to RM4.85 and CIMB Group Holdings Bhd added three sen to RM7.93. WCT Holdings Bhd gained four sen to 52 sen, GIIB Holdings Bhd increased 3.5 sen to 24 sen and Genetec Technology Bhd edged up one sen to 37 sen. Top gainers included UMS Integration Ltd, which rose 55 sen to RM8.55, UWC Bhd added 20 sen to RM5.93, Dutch Lady Milk Industries Bhd gained 18 sen to RM33.00 and Frontken Corporation Bhd was 12 sen firmer at RM4.69. Among the top losers, Hong Leong Industries Bhd slid 30 sen to RM19.00, Fraser & Neave Holdings Bhd gave up 22 sen to RM29.68, BLD Plantation Bhd eased 20 sen to RM14.80, while Petronas Dagangan Bhd fell 18 sen to RM20.14.

items.title: Former Bar chief urges protest over Jho Low pardon bid
items.summary: "The government, through the Attorney-General's Chambers, should write to the US Department of Justice raising concerns over the application and sending protest notes to the US government over any prospective move to consider Low's reprieve or pardon by the US president," he told the New Straits Times. Salim said US presidential pardon powers should not undermine efforts to bring Low to justice over one of the world's biggest financial fraud scandals. Salim said a US presidential pardon would not affect ongoing investigations or trials against Low in Malaysia.

items.title: Spain pushes ahead with social media, AI rules despite Big Tech pressure
items.summary: MADRID: Spain will push ahead with new rules to make social networks and AI safer despite intense lobbying from the tech industry, its digital transformation minister Oscar Lopez Agueda told Reuters. "The profit of four tech companies cannot come at the expense of the rights of millions," he said, adding that "powerful voices" were lobbying against proposed regulation that would curb high-risk AI systems or force companies to disclose how their social media algorithms work. His comments echoed those by European Commission President Ursula von der Leyen, who on Tuesday said the Commission was targeting addictive and harmful design practices by social media firms in its upcoming Digital Fairness Act.

items.title: From Compliance to Commitment: How Bursa Malaysia is shaping the ESG future
items.summary: In Malaysia, Bursa Malaysia has emerged as a key driver of this transformation, embedding ESG principles into its listing requirements, disclosure frameworks and market expectations. As the nation charts its path towards sustainable and inclusive growth, Bursa Malaysia's evolving ESG agenda reflects a broader shift: from compliance-driven reporting to a more meaningful commitment to long-term value creation. Why ESG Matters to Bursa Malaysia Bursa Malaysia's emphasis on ESG is not merely symbolic.

items.title: Gua Musang Form One student dies after collapsing at hostel
items.summary: KOTA BHARU, May 13 — A male Form One student died after reportedly being found unconscious at the hostel of Sekolah Menengah Paloh in Gua Musang yesterday. Gua Musang police chief Supt Sik Choon Foo said police received a report regarding the incident from the victim’s father at 8.53 am yesterday. Sik added that further police examination found no elements of crime in the incident.

items.title: Three more bodies found in Pulau Pangkor migrant boat tragedy
items.summary: LUMUT, May 13 — Three more bodies of women, believed to be victims of a boat capsize incident involving undocumented Indonesian migrants off Pulau Pangkor on Monday, have been found. Marine Police Region One Operations Command officer DSP Mazre Che Mahmod said the three bodies were discovered by Royal Malaysian Navy (RMN) vessel KD Sri Indera Sakti about 20 nautical miles from the location of the incident at around 6 pm yesterday. The third day of the SAR operation resumed at 7 am today, involving the Marine Police, RMN and the Malaysian Maritime Enforcement Agency (MMEA),” he said.

items.title: Maritime industry leader warns bill of lading mismatches can expose shipping sector to major risks
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 13 — A senior maritime industry executive has warned that mismatches involving bills of lading (BL) and physical cargo movement are emerging as a growing operational and regulatory risk in the global shipping and commodity trading sector. The shipping industry veteran, who has more than 30 years of experience, said the BL is closely tied to banking transactions, insurance arrangements, Customs declarations, cargo ownership representation and sanctions compliance. “The biggest risk for a shipping agent is when the physical cargo movement and the documentation stop aligning.

items.title: Form One student dies after found unconscious in school hostel
items.summary: GUA MUSANG: A 13-year-old male student died after he was reportedly found unconscious at the hostel of Sekolah Menengah Paloh in Gua Musang yesterday. District police chief Superintendent Sik Choon Foo said police received a report at 8.53am yesterday from a man informing them that his son was unconscious at the hostel. He said the school took the victim to the Chiku 3 Health Clinic for treatment.

items.title: CelcomDigi's Q1 earnings rise 8.85pct to RM418mil, revenue flat at RM3.21bil
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: CelcomDigi Bhd's net profit grew 8.85 per cent in the first quarter ended Mar 31, 2026 compared to a year ago on the back of effective cost discipline. The telco company posted a net profit of RM418 million, up from RM384 million in the same period last year. The company's service revenue grew 1.6 per cent year-on-year to RM2.7 billion supported by continued growth momentum in postpaid, home and fibre and enterprise solutions.

items.title: MK Land secures financial close for 29.99MW solar plant in Kedah
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Property developer MK Land Holdings Bhd has achieved financial close for the development of a 29.99 megawatts alternating current (MWac) solar photovoltaic (PV) plant in Kulim, Kedah. The project will be undertaken through the group's renewable energy subsidiary Citra Energies Sdn Bhd, under the government's Corporate Green Power Programme (CGPP) MK Land said the financial close enables the full-scale rollout of construction works for the solar PV facility and a 132-kilovolt loop-in-loop-out substation, supporting efforts to expand Malaysia's renewable energy capacity. The latest project builds on MK Land's earlier renewable energy venture through Solar Citra's 10.95 megawatts large-scale solar PV plan in Lembah Beriah, Perak, developed under Malaysia's large-scale solar 4 (LSS4) programme.

items.title: Former bank sales head fined RM90,000 for bribery, failure to report corrupt payments
items.summary: MELAKA: A former bank employee was fined a total of RM90,000 by the Ayer Keroh Sessions Court today after pleading guilty to 17 charges of bribery and failing to report corrupt payments. The charges were linked to housing loan referrals made during his tenure as a regional sales head. According to the charges, Mohd Rais accepted bribes totalling RM123,132.50 from a law firm through his wife's company account.

items.title: Philippine senator seeks military support to block ICC drug war arrest
items.summary: MANILA: Former Philippine president Rodrigo Duterte's chief drug war enforcer urged the military on Wednesday to stop government attempts to arrest and fly him to the Netherlands to stand trial on charges of crimes against humanity. Ronald Dela Rosa, a sitting senator and former police chief, began his third day holed up at the Senate building after its leadership stopped government efforts to serve an arrest warrant from the International Criminal Court over his role in Duterte's bloody drug war. I am appealing for peaceful support," Dela Rosa told reporters.

items.title: Penang Pas to lodge MACC report over alleged zakat fund misuse, governance issues
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN: Penang Pas will lodge a report with the Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) today over alleged governance irregularities and the handling of zakat funds involving the Penang Islamic religious authorities. He said the move was necessary following what he described as serious concerns involving public funds. Hafiz said the report would focus on several issues, including allegations of direct appointments without interview processes and claims that recruitment within Zakat MAINPP (Penang Islamic Religious Council) did not comply with eligibility requirements.

items.title: Federal Court adjourns Isa Samad’s bid to review reinstated corruption conviction
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, May 13 — The Federal Court today adjourned the hearing of former Federal Land Development Authority (Felda) chairman Tan Sri Mohd Isa Abdul Samad’s application seeking leave to review a previous Federal Court ruling that reinstated his corruption conviction, six-year prison sentence and RM15.45 million fine. Chief Judge of Malaya Datuk Seri Hashim Hamzah, who chaired a three-member bench, adjourned the hearing scheduled today after allowing the defence’s request to view the court recordings and transcription (CRT) of the open court proceedings held on Feb 5 and 10 this year. Also sitting on the bench were Chief Judge of Sabah and Sarawak Datuk Azizah Nawawi and Federal Court judge Datuk Collin Lawrence Sequerah.

items.title: Tengku Hassanal reaches Mount Kinabalu summit
items.summary: KUNDASANG: Tengku Mahkota of Pahang Tengku Hassanal Ibrahim Alam Shah reached the summit of Mount Kinabalu at 8.45am today. Tengku Hassanal and his entourage began their final ascent at 3.07am after arriving at Panalaban at 3.45pm yesterday. Julaimin said weather conditions were ideal, with temperatures at 9°C, ensuring a safe and smooth expedition.

items.title: Sarawak plans to gazette Three Sisters waterfall as protected area
items.summary: KUCHING: The Sarawak Forestry Corporation (SFC) is preparing a proposal to gazette Keluan Hill National Park in Baram as a protected area, the state assembly was told today. Deputy Minister for Urban Planning, Land Administration and Environment Datuk Len Talif Salleh said the proposed area covers about 1,619ha and includes the Three Sisters Waterfall. The gazettement of the area as a Totally Protected Area will bring benefits in terms of well-managed and sustainable governance, protection of natural resources, and biodiversity conservation, he said.

items.title: Roofs torn off, trees uprooted, and power cut as whirlwind batters MPL Housing in Labuan, one injured
items.summary: LABUAN, May 13 — A residential area at MPL Housing near Tanjung Aru here was hit by a tornado-like storm last night, damaging 33 houses and leaving one man injured. The incident, which occurred at about 9.40 pm, saw strong winds ripping off rooftops, damaging several vehicles and uprooting large trees in nearby areas. Several roads were blocked by scattered debris and fallen objects.

items.title: New routes along Pan Borneo highway to open from July, easing traffic around Kota Kinabalu
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, May 13 — Nine work packages under Phase 1A of the Sabah Pan Borneo Highway project are expected to be fully completed and opened to motorists this year, Deputy Works Minister Datuk Seri Ahmad Maslan said. “Previously, four out of the 15 work packages under Phase 1A were completed in stages between 2023 and 2025. Phase 1 of the Sabah Pan Borneo Highway project comprises 35 work packages covering a total alignment of 706km from Sindumin to Tawau.

items.title: Pahang set to gazette 524 hectares in Bera for permanent forest reserves
items.summary: KUANTAN, May 13 — The Pahang government has identified several forest areas to be proposed as new Permanent Forest Reserves (HSK) to balance development needs with environmental conservation, the State Assembly was told today. “The total area of Pahang is 3.60 million hectares, while the size of the Permanent Forest Reserves currently stands at 1.589 million hectares, representing 44.13 per cent of the state’s total land area. Wan Rosdy said the state government would continue ensuring that any HSK area degazetted for economic development purposes would be replaced with another forest area of equivalent value.

items.title: 1MDB task force chief says Jho Low should not be pardoned amid reported US clemency bid
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 13 — Fugitive Malaysian financier Low Taek ‌Jho, a central figure in the multibillion-dollar scandal at state fund 1Malaysia Development Berhad (1MDB), should not be pardoned, the chairman of the 1MDB taskforce said today, after a report said Low ‌was seeking clemency from US President Donald Trump. Low, widely known as Jho Low, faces multiple charges, including corruption and money laundering in the United States and Malaysia, for the key role he allegedly played in the misappropriation of at least US$4.5 billion from 1MDB. A White House official said Low’s request was not currently on the White House’s radar, the report said.

items.title: Penang tightens sanitation, rodent monitoring at Swettenham Pier amid Hantavirus concerns
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN, May 13 — The Penang Port Commission (PPC) has strengthened precautionary and health surveillance measures at the Swettenham Pier Cruise Terminal (SPCT) following international reports of a cluster of Hantavirus cases linked to a cruise ship. PPC chairman Datuk Yeoh Soon Hin said although no Hantavirus infections have been reported in Malaysia so far, the commission is treating the matter seriously to protect passengers, ship crew and the local community by strengthening ship sanitation inspections and rodent‑infestation monitoring within the terminal. Yeoh said infectious‑disease surveillance activities have been intensified, including event‑based surveillance, to detect any unusual incidents quickly.

items.title: Fish catch in Teluk Kumbar, Batu Maung remains steady despite Silicon Island reclamation, says Penang govt
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN, May 13 — The Penang government has refuted claims that the development of the Silicon Island project has adversely affected the fisheries sector, saying fish landings by fishermen in Teluk Kumbar and Batu Maung have shown a stable trend. State Rural Development, Agrotechnology, Food Security and Entrepreneurship Committee chairman Datuk Rashidi Zinol said the total declared fish landings in 2023 stood at 472.44 tonnes, while in 2025, the figure increased to 496.57 tonnes. Feedback obtained also showed that fishermen in the Silicon Island project area did not experience a situation of no marine catch due to the works,” he said.

items.title: New ‘Option to Purchase’ rule could help reduce abandoned housing projects, says developers’ group
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 13 — The proposed “Option to Purchase” (OTP) clause under the new housing law could provide greater protection for prospective home buyers while helping developers to better gauge genuine market interest and reduce the risk of abandoned projects, said the Real Estate and Housing Developers’ Association (Rehda). President Datuk Ho Hon Sang said under the proposed clause, both purchasers and developers can withdraw from the transaction before the sale and purchase agreement (SPA) is signed. Nga recently announced that his ministry is studying the OTP mechanism to be introduced under the proposed Real Property Development Bill, to reduce the risk of sick, delayed, and abandoned housing projects, while providing flexibility for homebuyers and developers.

items.title: Lim Guan Eng urges govt to maintain BUDI95, calls for banks to fund RM5b MSME support instead of targeting T20
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 13 — The government should maintain the Budi Madani RON95 (Budi95) fuel subsidy scheme for all Malaysians and instead require banks to contribute more directly to MSME support, DAP Bagan MP Lim Guan Eng said. In a statement today, Lim said Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim could “avoid accusations of double standards” by targeting the banking sector rather than excluding the T20 income group from fuel subsidies. Lim also urged the government to instead engage banks, which he said continue to post strong profits, to support micro, small and medium enterprises (MSMEs) through RM5 billion in interest-free and collateral-free loans of RM50,000 to RM100,000, as well as a one-year interest moratorium on existing MSME loans.

items.title: Sultan Ibrahim receives report on mosque projects in Johor Bahru, Kluang, Mersing and Kulai
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, May 13 — His Majesty Sultan Ibrahim, King of Malaysia, has received a report on the construction of five new mosques in Johor from state mufti Datuk Yahya Ahmad. Also present were Johor Islamic Religious Department director Datuk Nasri Md Ali and Johor Islamic Religious Council chief executive officer Datuk Mohd Radzi Mohd Amin. Also present was state Lands and Mines director Datuk Mohammed Shakib Ali.

items.title: Home minister hits back at claims he gave Malaysian citizenship to Chinese nationals, calls it slander
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 13 — Home Minister Datuk Seri Saifuddin Nasution Ismail has dismissed allegations that he arbitrarily grants Malaysian citizenship to Chinese nationals, describing the claims as slanderous and malicious attempts to incite public sentiment and create confusion. Saifuddin Nasution urged the public not to be easily influenced by false narratives and to verify information through authentic and reliable sources. “I also call on irresponsible parties spreading these slanderous claims and false information to immediately stop such unethical actions and instead support the efforts of the Madani Government in addressing current challenges, including the global supply crisis affecting the people and the country,” he said.

items.title: Explosion near Unifil headquarters in Lebanon damages Malaysia House, no casualties reported
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 13 — An explosion believed to involve an unidentified drone occurred near the United Nations Interim Force in Lebanon (Unifil) headquarters in Naqoura yesterday, causing minor damage to a Malaysian military rest facility, the Defence Ministry said. Defence Minister Datuk Seri Mohamed Khaled Nordin, in a statement, said the incident took place at about 5.30pm Malaysian time near the Unifil headquarters perimeter in southern Lebanon. He said the explosion “is believed to have occurred near the Unifil headquarters perimeter fence and caused minor damage to both the exterior and interior sections of Malaysia House.” Malaysia House is a rest facility for five Malaysian Armed Forces officers serving as staff officers under the Unifil headquarters in Naqoura.

items.title: Pedestrian, believed hit by vehicle, found dead in two-metre drain in Klang
items.summary: SHAH ALAM, May 13 — A man was killed after he was believed to have been struck by a vehicle and thrown into a two-metre-deep drain along Jalan Kapar, Batu 5, Klang, early today. Selangor Fire and Rescue Department (JBPM) Operations Division assistant director Ahmad Mukhlis Mukhtar said the department received a call on the incident at 3.23 am. “Seven officers and personnel in one fire engine from the Kapar station were dispatched to the scene,” he said in a statement.

items.title: Police recover stolen jewellery after gold shop heist in Jerantut, two suspected robbers held in Chow Kit
items.summary: KUANTAN, May 13 — Police have arrested two men suspected of involvement in a jewellery theft case at a gold shop in Jerantut last April 28. Jerantut Acting Police Chief DSP Arshad Ayob said the suspects, aged 23 and 63, were detained at about 4pm last Monday at separate locations in Chow Kit, Kuala Lumpur. On May 1, media reports stated that police were tracking down a man suspected of stealing jewellery worth nearly RM26,000 from a gold shop in Jerantut.

items.title: Malaysia Airlines, Firefly offer RM499 festive fares to East Malaysia, add 62 extra flights
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 13 — Malaysia Aviation Group (MAG), through Malaysia Airlines and Firefly, is supporting increased travel demand ahead of the upcoming Hari Raya Aidiladha, Kaamatan and Gawai celebrations with subsidised fares on selected East Malaysia routes. In a statement today, MAG said Malaysia Airlines will operate an additional 62 flights across high-demand routes including Kota Bharu, Labuan, Tawau, Sandakan, Kuching, Sibu and Miri to accommodate the festive travel surge. The group said both Malaysia Airlines and Firefly will support the government’s festive travel initiative by capping base fares at RM499 one-way on selected East Malaysia routes.

items.title: Fire kills wheelchair-bound daughter, leaves 71-year-old mother hurt in Parit Buntar house
items.summary: IPOH, May 13 — A disabled woman was killed, while her mother was injured, after their double-storey home at Parit 310, Bagan Tiang, near Parit Buntar, caught fire early today. He said the fire destroyed about 70 per cent of the double-storey house and also damaged a Proton Iriz car. In a separate incident, Sabarodzi said firefighters from the Bagan Serai station retrieved a man from a drain after the car he was driving skidded along Jalan Besar Bagan Serai-Taiping near Bagan Serai at about 2.30 am today.

items.title: Malaysia smashes first-quarter tourism record with 10.6 million arrivals
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 13 — Malaysia recorded a new tourism milestone in the first quarter of 2026, welcoming more than 10.6 million international visitors — the highest ever for the period and the second consecutive year the country has crossed the 10 million mark in the first three months of the year. In a statement today, Tourism, Arts and Culture Minister Datuk Seri Tiong King Sing said Malaysia received 10,647,200 international visitors between January and March this year, a 5.4 per cent increase from the same period last year. Malaysia also recorded its highest-ever monthly tourist arrival figure in February this year, when international arrivals reached 3,472,557.

items.title: Malaysia to join BRICS foreign ministers’ meeting in New Delhi this week
items.summary: HYDERABAD, May 13 — Malaysia will participate in the BRICS foreign ministers’ meeting being held in New Delhi on May 14 and15. Foreign Minister Datuk Seri Mohamad Hasan is expected to arrive in the Indian capital on Wednesday, a Malaysian diplomat at the High Commission told Bernama. The meeting will be chaired by India’s External Affairs Minister Subrahmanyam Jaishankar, ministry spokesman Randhir Jaiswal told a media briefing in New Delhi.

items.title: Death toll rises in Pulau Pangkor migrant boat tragedy after rescuers recover four bodies, 10 still missing
items.summary: LUMUT, May 13 — Four bodies were recovered in the waters off Pulau Pangkor today during the second day of a search and rescue operation for victims of a capsized boat carrying illegal immigrants. Perak Malaysian Maritime Enforcement Agency (MMEA) director, Maritime Captain Mohamad Shukri Khotob, said the bodies were spotted by the Marine Police Force (PPM) at 2 pm. “Two PPM boats found the bodies floating some 13 nautical miles from Pulau Talang, near Segari.

items.title: Air Selangor contractor found lifeless in Taman Sentosa drain, Klang police say died likely from heart attack
items.summary: SHAH ALAM, May 13 — A man whose body was found in a drain at Taman Sentosa, Klang, yesterday afternoon is believed to have died of a heart attack. Klang Selatan district police chief ACP Lim Jit Huey said police received a report on the discovery of the local man’s body at 1.28 pm. “At 2.05 pm, an ambulance from Tengku Ampuan Rahimah Hospital in Klang arrived at the scene and confirmed the man had died there.

items.title: Negeri Sembilan closes Ulu Bendul Bendul Eco Park till May 24 over suspected leptospirosis scare
items.summary: SEREMBAN, May 13 — The Ulu Bendul Forest Eco Park in Kuala Pilah has been temporarily closed to all activities until May 24 following reports of suspected leptospirosis, or rat urine disease, cases in the area. Negeri Sembilan Forestry Department director Mohd Yussainy Md Yusop said the closure would allow sanitation, thorough cleaning and water quality monitoring to be carried out to ensure visitors’ safety. Mohd Yussainy said all water activities at the eco park are prohibited throughout the closure period.

items.title: Jho Low seeks Trump pardon in bid to clear US criminal charges
items.summary: NEW YORK, May 13 — Malaysian financier Low Taek ‌Jho, widely known as Jho Low and now a ‌fugitive, has sought a pardon from US President Donald Trump, the Wall Street Journal (WSJ) reported yesterday. Reuters could not immediately verify the report. — Reuters
